journal_entry.description: Purchase merchandise on account with freight, insurance, storage, and delivery costs
journal_entry.rationale: Under IAS 2, inventory cost includes purchase price plus costs necessary to bring inventory to its present location and condition. Freight to warehouse ($120,000) and shipping insurance ($30,000) are capitalizable as they are incurred before the inventory is ready for sale. Post-acquisition warehouse storage ($20,000) and outbound delivery freight ($50,000) are period costs expensed as incurred because they occur after inventory is ready for sale. HST at 13% applies to all taxable supplies in Ontario.

output_debit_classifier.reason: Merchandise purchase on account increases inventory (asset) and accounts payable (liability). Inbound freight and insurance are capitalized to inventory per IAS 2. Post-acquisition storage and outbound delivery are operating expenses. Cash payments reduce cash asset. HST 13% is recoverable on all purchases.
